intro: Your alarm goes off. You don't remember setting it that early.

scenarios.title: The Assignment
scenarios.subtitle: A week out. The clock is ticking.
scenarios.prompt: You check your phone. There's a reminder you set for yourself. Big assignment, due next week.

choices.text: Get up and open the laptop. Might as well start while it's quiet.
choices.reaction: The screen lights up. It's early.

choices.text: Note it, put the phone down. I'll deal with it after class.
choices.reaction: The phone goes dark. Back to sleep.

choices.text: Clear the notification and roll over. Future me can handle it.
choices.reaction: Future you is going to love that.

choices.text: I don't remember setting that reminder either.
choices.reaction: Cool. Cool cool cool.

scenarios.title: The Difficult Concept
scenarios.subtitle: When the material just isn't clicking.
scenarios.prompt: Class just ended. You were completely lost the entire time. Everyone else seemed fine.

choices.text: Reread the slides after class and dig around online until it clicks.
choices.reaction: You open the slides. Start from the top.

choices.text: Find a YouTube video. Someone out there explains it better.
choices.reaction: Three million results. One of them has to make sense.

choices.text: Text a classmate or show up to office hours.
choices.reaction: Message sent.

choices.text: Skip it. If it's on the exam, that's a problem for later.
choices.reaction: Bold strategy.

scenarios.title: The Group Project
scenarios.subtitle: Everyone's favorite assignment.
scenarios.prompt: The professor puts you in a group of four for a project worth 30% of your grade. You don't know any of them. The first meeting is tonight.

choices.text: Make a group chat and send an agenda before anyone else does.
choices.reaction: You're typing before anyone else has saved the contact.

choices.text: Show up, volunteer for a section, and handle my part.
choices.reaction: In and out. Clean.

choices.text: Throw out a bunch of ideas in the first meeting. Figure out the rest later.
choices.reaction: The ideas are flying. The plan is not.

choices.text: Show up, nod along, and hope someone else takes the lead.
choices.reaction: Participation: technically present.

scenarios.title: The Exam
scenarios.subtitle: It's that time.
scenarios.prompt: A few weeks in. You've got a big exam coming up and your notes are a mess.

choices.text: Make study guides and flashcards. Lock in early.
choices.reaction: The highlighters come out.

choices.text: Reread my notes a couple times and hope the main ideas stuck.
choices.reaction: Familiar enough. Probably.

choices.text: Cram the night before. It's stressful but it works. Mostly.
choices.reaction: The coffee's on. Sleep is a suggestion.

choices.text: Wing it and see what happens.
choices.reaction: Brave.

scenarios.title: The Exam Room
scenarios.subtitle: No turning back now.
scenarios.prompt: You're sitting in the exam. You know some stuff, blank on others.

choices.text: Work through what I know first, come back to the rest.
choices.reaction: Pen moving. Skip, skip, come back.

choices.text: Start from the top and go straight through.
choices.reaction: Question one. Here we go.

choices.text: Spend too long on the hard ones. Run out of time.
choices.reaction: You look at the clock. Yikes.

choices.text: Write something for everything. Partial credit is the goal.
choices.reaction: Partial credit never hurt anybody.

scenarios.title: The Blank Page
scenarios.subtitle: Cursor blinking. Nothing written.
scenarios.prompt: A few days later. You have a paper due Friday. You're staring at an empty document. The cursor is blinking.

choices.text: Start with an outline. Section by section, I'll get through it.
choices.reaction: Section one. Okay. You can do this.

choices.text: Just start typing and see what comes out.
choices.reaction: Words on the page. Not good ones yet. But words.

choices.text: Write the intro last. I never know how to start.
choices.reaction: Nobody knows how to start these anyway.

choices.text: Write a sentence. Delete it. Write another one. Delete that too.
choices.reaction: The cursor blinks. It's very patient.

scenarios.title: The Career Fair
scenarios.subtitle: The question everyone keeps asking.
scenarios.prompt: You're at a career fair. Someone at a booth asks "so what are you interested in?"

choices.text: I tell them exactly what I'm going for. I've got a plan.
choices.reaction: They nod. They've got a pamphlet for that.

choices.text: Give a general answer. I know the direction but not the details.
choices.reaction: Close enough. They hand you a card.

choices.text: Say something vague and grab a brochure. I'm still figuring it out.
choices.reaction: Into the tote bag it goes.

choices.text: Walk past most of the booths. I'm not ready for this conversation.
choices.reaction: Headphones in. Keep moving.

scenarios.title: The Overwhelm
scenarios.subtitle: Everything at once.
scenarios.prompt: It's midterms week. You've got two exams, a paper, and the group project all due within the next five days. You sit down at your desk.

choices.text: Pull out my planner and map out every hour between now and Friday.
choices.reaction: Friday. That's the finish line.

choices.text: Figure out what's worth the most points and start there.
choices.reaction: Triage mode.

choices.text: Open everything at once and bounce between them.
choices.reaction: Twelve tabs. Three documents. Zero progress.

choices.text: Stare at the wall for a minute. This is a lot.
choices.reaction: Just a minute.

scenarios.title: The Spark
scenarios.subtitle: Something catches your eye.
scenarios.prompt: Midterms are over. You're scrolling through your phone and see someone your age who built something cool. An app, a project, a brand. You think "I want to learn how to do that."

choices.text: Look up a course or tutorial and start that night.
choices.reaction: Tab one of many.

choices.text: Save the post and tell myself I'll get to it over break.
choices.reaction: Saved. Right next to the other 47.

choices.text: Watch a few videos about it and move on.
choices.reaction: The algorithm takes it from here.

choices.text: Think about it for a while, then keep scrolling.
choices.reaction: The thought lingers. The thumb doesn't.
